output.final_deliverable:
1) Exact H1 text from https://example.com/: Not available in the merged results. (The search/result metadata shows the page title “Example Domain,” but the visit result did not provide the exact H1 text.)

2) Exact domain of the external link from https://example.com/: iana.org (example.com’s extracted external link: https://iana.org/domains/example)

3) One URL citation for each visited page:
   - https://example.com/
   - https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved

However, the merged results do not contain the page’s exact H1 text. Citations used: https://example.com/ and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved.

results.title: Html how to make H1, H2, etc as links? - Stack Overflow
results.description: All major crawlers are intelligent enough to respect a <h1> tag. Providing your anchor tag is not malicious or erroneous. Basically I don't have any idea what you're asking here.

fe7b47b9-1f5f-449d-b889-2ba5d124f90b.title: Visit https://example.com and extract H1 text, external link domain, and one citation URL

details.intent: Retrieve the full page content of example.com and extract exact H1 text, identify the exact domain of the external link present on the page, and provide the page URL as a citation.
target_facts: Exact H1 text as it appears in the page (including exact capitalization and punctuation)
Exact URL of the external link present on example.com (the href attribute)
Domain extracted from that external link (e.g., 'iana.org')
One URL citation for the visited page (the page's canonical or visited URL)
extraction_plan: Load full HTML/text of https://example.com/ (minimum 6000 chars if available).
Locate the first-level heading tag (<h1>) and record its exact inner text.
Find anchor elements (<a>) that link to external domains; capture the href of the external link referenced on the page.
Parse the captured href to extract the exact domain (no path or scheme).
Return the visited page URL as the citation.
verification_steps: Confirm the extracted H1 exactly matches the text inside the page's <h1> element.
Confirm the external href is present in the page content and is not a relative link to the same domain.
Confirm domain extraction removes scheme and path, yielding only the registered domain.
Provide the exact URL used for the visit as the citation.
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Directly visiting the page provides authoritative extraction of heading and link href values; extracting the domain from the href ensures precise answer to the user's domain question.
details.parent_goal: Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.
details.original_goal: Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.
